title: THIRTY-SEVEN WINES
description: Thirty-Seven Wines looks quiet. On a Saturday Morning soft with the promise of autumn.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: AMISTA VINEYARDS
description: Finding Friendship In A Glass Of Wine: My Visit To Amista Vineyar
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: GLORIA FERRER CAVE & VINEYARDS
description: Happy Wednesday, Everyone! There is something uniquely magical about a wine tasting on a weekday. The crowds are thinner; the servers are more personal and the pace is slower.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: STUHLMULLER VINEYARDS
description: A SLICE OF SONOMA PARADISE: A VISIT TO STUHLMULLER VINEYARDS
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: TOP SEVEN STATES THAT HAVE WINERIES
description: HERE IS THE CURRENT TOP SEVEN STATES THAT HAVE WINERIES IN NORTH AMERICA: CALIFORNIA-4,646 OREGON-868 WASHINGTON-821 TEXAS-521 NEW YORK-450 PENNSYLVANIA-401 VIRGINIA-395
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: BROWN ESTATE
description: The First Black Owned Winery In Napa
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: SONOMA COAST VINEYARD
description: The highlight was the walk to the edge of the estate vineyard. Standing between a row of gnarled Pinot vines, the ocean roaring below, the connection was visceral. You could see the struggle and resilience in the low-trained vines. You could taste the ocean in the wine. This is what terroir truly means-not just soil, but sky, wind and spirit.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: WINERY 1912
description: The past weekend, I traded the familiar for something truly special. A trip to Winery 1912 in Sedona. For anyone who thinks great America wine only comes from California, this place is a delightful and necessary revelation.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: OAK CREEK VINEYARDS & WINERY
description: I just returned from a delightful escape to the Arizona wine country, right in the heart of the Verde Valley. Nestled in the small town of Cornville, just 20 minutes south of Sedona and less than two hours from Phoenix, Lies Oak Creek Vineyards & Winery.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: OAK CREEK VINEYARDS & WINERY
description: A boutique, family-owned business founded by Deb Wahl in 2002. Located in Cornville, AZ, nestled in the scenic Verde Valley near Sedona. Approximately 10 acres with a deep aquifer providing water. They grow Syrah, Merlot, Zinfandel, and Chardonnay.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: CAPRIO CELLARS
description: During a recent trip to Washington State's wine country, my quest for a genuine tasting experience led me to the unpretentious, exceptional Caprio Cellars. Nestled in the Walla Walla Valley, this winery has engineered a hospitality experience so innovative and welcoming that it redefines what a visit to a vineyard can be.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: OLALLA VINEYARE & WINERY
description: Off the beaten path, finding a secret gem at Olalla Vineyard. One recent trip, I discovered that the most memorable wine-tasting experiences are often hidden down quiet, wooded roads. Tucked away in the Kitsap Peninsula, Olalla Vineyard & Winery is one of Washington's best-kept secrets, offering a perfect blend of rustic charm, warn hospitality, and distinctive wines that make it well worth the journey.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: ABBE VINEYARD & WINERY
description: In the heart of Federal Way, a stone's throw from the buzz of Pacific Highway and the thrills of Wild Waves, lies a small piece of Washington wine country that feels a world away. Abbe Vineyard Winery is a testament to passion over pretense, a boutique winery where the journey is just as rewarding as the destination.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: BENSON VINEYARDS ESTATE WINERY
description: The moment I turned off the main road and onto the winding drive of Benson Vineyard Estate, the world seemed to slow down. Rolling hills of groomed vines stretch toward the horizon, and the air carries a subtle, sweet earthiness. My visit last month wasn't just a wine tasting; it was an immersion into the craft and care behind every bottle.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: CASCADE CLIFFS WINERY
description: This is one of the original wineries of the Columbia River George, with a history dating back to the mid-1980s. The estate vineyard has a compelling story. It's situated 260 feet above the Columbia River on basalt rock, where light reflects off the water and heat radiates from the north-facing cliffs, creating a unique microclimate. The land itself is a key part of the wine's identity.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: IRVINE & ROBERTS VINEYARDS
description: A Journey to the Summit, Discovering Elegance at Irvine & Roberts Vineyards
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: ANKENY VINEYARD
description: Founded in 1980, located in the Willamette Valley, near Salem Oregon. On a 50-acre estate, owned by Rod Phelan and winemaker Drew Voit. Tucked into the hills with views of the Ankeny National Wildlife Refuge, creating a peaceful, rustic setting.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: LAVELLE VINEYARDS
description: Established in 1994, it's the oldest bonded winery in the Southern Willamette Valley. Founded by Doug Lavelle after a 30-year corporate career, now run with his son Matthew, the winemaker.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: DEL RIO VINEYARD ESTATE
description: Last weekend, I discovered that the perfect Oregon wine tasting pairs award-winning wines with a dash of ghostly mystery. My visit to Del Rio Vineyard Estate was an unforgettable blend of rich history, stunning scenery, and exceptional wines. The adventure begins the moment you arrive. The charming tasting room is housed in the historic Rock Point Stage Hotel, originally built in 1864. You can feel the warmth and rustic charm of those early Oregon days as you walk through the door. The atmosphere is so authentic, you almost expect to see stagecoaches arriving outside. This place has more stories than just those of weary travelers. The tasting room has a famed, friendly resident ghost named Hattie Haymond. Her portrait hangs on the wall, and the staff have plenty of eerie tales to share, from moving furniture to late-night appearances in the windows. It's a piece of local lore that makes your tasting feel wonderfully unique.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: OLALLA VINEYARD AND WINERY
description: This week, my adventures took me off the beaten path to a true hidden gem: Olalla Vineyard and Winery. If you're tired of the crowds and looking for a slice of rustic, authentic paradise, you're going to want to read this.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: CHATEAU STE. MICHELLE
description: Washington State's oldest winery, founded in 1934, Woodinville, WA. A stunning French-style chateau surrounded by 87 acres of park-like grounds. Pioneered vinification of Riesling and Cold Creek Vineyard grapes. Helped establish Washington as a world-class wine region.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: MONTE SCARLATTO ESTATE WINERY
description: 10 acres on Red Mountain, planted vineyard in 2007 and first vintage in 2011. Scarlet Mountain in Italian means, honoring mother's heritage.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: SCRIBNER BEND VINEYARDS
description: Scribner Bend vineyard tells their story not in pages, but in rows of vines curling along the Sacramento River. I spent a recent afternoon there, and the theme was unmistakable: this is a place where every glass comes with a chapter of family, river soil, and quiet dedication.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: VINO DE ORO
description: They say the best treasures are hidden, and Vino de Oro is proof. Tucked away on a sun-drenched hillside, this family-owned winery feels less like a business and more like a secret you've been let in on. From the moment you step onto the property, the pace of the world slows down, replaced by the quiet hum of bees and the scent of warm earth and aging oak.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: HERINGER ESTATE WINERY
description: There's a special feeling that comes with visiting a family farm-a sense of authenticity, warmth, and connection to the land. That's exactly the experience we strive to create every day at Heringer Estate Winery.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: PROVENANCE VINEYARD
description: The Provenance Vineyards brand has a complex and fascinating history that is central to its story today. Founded in 1999 with a winery and tasting room in Rutherford, Napa Valley. Focused on single-vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon from esteemed sites like the Hewitt Vineyard. The Lombardi family acquired the Provenance Vineyard brand in November of 2021. The company's headquarters are now listed as Lodi, California.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: CHATEAU DAVELL WINERY
description: From the moment I drove up the winding gravel path, I was struck by the sheer beauty of the place. The rustic-chic tasting room, with its sprawling patio overlooking the vineyards, feels both elegant and welcoming. You aren't just a customer here: you're a guest.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: GOLD HILL VINEYARD
description: They've been growing grapes here since the Gold Rush-some of these wines might have witnessed the '49ers panning in the creeks below.'
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: SIERRA VISTA VINEYARD & WINERY
description: A Taste of the High Country: An Afternoon at Sierra Vineyard & Winery
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: LAVA CAP WINERY
description: The best wines aren't made in the cellar-they're grown in the soil.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: ELEMENT 79 VINEYARDS
description: The name "Element 79" is the scientific term for gold on the periodic table, and it's a nod to the vineyard's unique history. Located in the heart of California's Gold Country, the land itself is steeped in the legacy of the 49er. Instead of panning for fleeting nuggets, the owners are cultivating a different kind of treasure.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: STARFIELD VINEYARDS
description: Starfield Vineyards is a place that stays with you. It's more than just a tasting, it's an experience for the eyes, palate and soul. It's the feeling of the city stress melting away as you turn onto the property, the taste of a robust red blend as you look out over the lake and the joy of an unplanned bonus pour that turns a simple getaway into a cherished memory.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: CASINO MINE RANCH WINERY
description: The name had intrigued me for months. It was a contradiction, a puzzle. Casino promised luck and quick riches: Mine spoke of slow, back-breaking labor: Ranch suggested a pastoral simplicity: and Winery evoked refinement and patience. I had to see what kind of place could be all four.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: CARTER ESTATE WINERY
description: With over 112 acres of vines, a luxurious Carter Estate is an upscale resort and winery. 2025 Awards Winemaking Challenge* 2016 Carter Estate Winery Brut: Best Vintage Brut: Gold* 2023 Carter Estate Winery Les Nobles Private Reserve: Gold* 2017 Carter Estate Winery Blanc de Blanc: Silver
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: MIRAMONTE WINERY
description: Started in 2001, Miramonte Winery sits at an elevation of 1,400 feet. You want to enjoy sites of the Temecula Valley and vineyards; this is the place to visit. One of the few wineries where no reservations are required any day of the week. Live music offered on Friday and Saturday nights, everything from Reggae to 1990s soft rock.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: TEMECULA VALLEY
description: California is the leading wine producer in the United States, over 85% of wine is made in California. The state that has mild climate along the coast, and diverse soils- which is a very big advantage for growing grapes. Napa Valley, Sonoma County, Monterey area, San Diego County and Temecula Valley. Nearly 50 wineries are located in the Temecula Valley that produces grapes from grand estates with lavish tasting rooms, with wineries that has great vineyard views.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: GRANITE LION CELLARS
description: A visit to Granite Lion Cellars offers more than just wine tasting-it's a full sensory experience. The vineyard is nestled in the sunny foothills of Jamul cradled against the sun and fed by cool evening sea breezes, which is ideal growing conditions for their grapes. Located 19 miles east of San Diego.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: THE DANCING FOX WINERY & BREWERY LODI, CA
description: The Dancing Fox is a true family passion project, founded by Gregg and Colleen Lewis. The business is deeply rooted in Lodi, CA. Operating from a century-old brick building on Schools Street in downtown Lod
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: TASTE MORGAN-MORGAN WINERY
description: The estate vineyard in the Santa Lucia Highlands, developed in 1996. It has been Certified Organic since 2005 and is the only certified organic vineyard in the Santa Lucia Highlands.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: FOLKTALE WINERY & VINEYARD
description: SIP, SAVOR AND WELCOME TO FOLKTALE WINERY.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: BERINGER VINEYARDS
description: Tastings at the 1886 historic Rhine House. Take a visit in the historical home and savor one of the best Beringer experiences.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: CASTILLO DI AMAROSA CALISTOGA
description: Visiting Castello di Amorosa is more than a winery-it is an experience. Beyond the Drawbridge, the real magic, knights, wine, barrel rooms and it's Napa's most famous Castle worth visiting.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: Rombauer Vineyards Plymouth CA
description: Your Personal Invite to Rombauer Vineyards
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: WEST COAST WINERIES
description: Hello and a very warm welcome, if you're reading this, it's likely because you share our passion for the world of West Coast Wine Tours. Maybe you love the bold Cabernets of Napa, the ethereal Pinots of the Willamette Valley.Think of this newsletter as your regular invitation to explore hidden gems in the wine world. We're not just listing tasting notes, sharing stories. We'll introduce you to the visionary winemakers, walk the rocky vineyards, and uncork the bottles that tell the true story of West coast Wine Tours.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: Napa Valley Food
description: An Evening Visiting Hilltop Dining Room. My dinner at the Hilltop Dining Room was truly memorable experience, blending exquisite cuisine with an atmosphere of understated elegance. Upon arrival, the first thing that stood out to me was the view. The dining room lives up to its name, offering panoramic, floor-to-ceiling windows that showcased a stunning sunset over the valley. The service was impeccable from start to finish. Was greeted warmly and seated promptly, even when the restaurant recommends reservations. Indoor and outdoor seating is provided, but with the evening wind blowing, inside was just fine. Starter: Began with the Seared Scallops with cauliflower purée. The scallops were perfectly cooked-caramelized on the outside and tender within. The purée was smooth and velvety, providing a wonderful contrast in texture and subtle, sweet flavor that complements the scallops beautifully.
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS

title: Bed And Barrel
description: Clearlake Oaks 500 Old Long Valley Rd 95423
author: WEST COAST WINE TOURS
